touches.ai_summary: Sent LinkedIn connection request to Dylan Takahashi with brief note about CS handoff challenges. No response.
Cold call attempt to Dylan's direct line. No answer, left brief voicemail introducing our CS handoff automation solution.
Follow-up LinkedIn message referencing common pain points in sales-to-CS handoff. Dylan responded expressing interest in learning more.

questions_asked.question: How does this differ from what Clari offers?
questions_asked.answer: Explained our focus on post-sale automation vs Clari's revenue operations focus, offered to show specific CS handoff workflows.

touches.ai_summary: Sent email with calendar link and one-pager on CS handoff automation. Dylan didn't respond but call was already scheduled from LinkedIn.

Qualification call with Dylan. He's 3 months into VP of CS role, dealing with messy handoffs from sales. Currently tracking in spreadsheets. Team of 9 CS managers. Interested in automation but budget conscious.

questions_asked.question: What's the biggest pain point in your current sales-to-CS handoff process?
questions_asked.answer: Information gets lost between systems. Sales uses Salesforce, CS uses a mix of tools. We're rebuilding context on every new customer.

questions_asked.question: Have you looked at other solutions?
questions_asked.answer: We're already paying for Clari but honestly not using the handoff features much. Feels built for sales ops, not CS.

questions_asked.question: What's the typical implementation timeline?
questions_asked.answer: Usually 2-3 weeks for core handoff automation, can extend for custom workflows.

objections: We're already spending a lot on tools, need to see clear ROI
next_steps: Dylan to map out current handoff workflow
Schedule discovery call for next week

touches.ai_summary: Discovery call focused on Dylan's handoff workflow. He walked through their current process: 7-step manual checklist, avg 3 hours per handoff, frequent data gaps. Interested in seeing demo of automations.

questions_asked.question: Who else is involved in evaluating CS tools?
questions_asked.answer: Honestly, it's mostly on me. Our CRO is focused on sales headcount. I have budget authority for CS tooling under $10k.

questions_asked.question: How are you measuring handoff quality today?
questions_asked.answer: We're not, really. Just tracking how many support tickets come in the first 30 days. It's not great.

next_steps: Dylan to document 2-3 recent problem handoffs as examples

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email with demo prep guide and questions about tech stack. Dylan confirmed Salesforce, Zendesk, and Slack integrations needed. Requested pricing estimate for 9 seats.

questions_asked.question: Can you give me a ballpark on pricing before the demo?
questions_asked.answer: Shared indicative range of $8-12k for 9 seats depending on features. Dylan acknowledged, didn't push back.

touches.ai_summary: Technical eval call with Dylan and two CS managers. Walked through automations platform, showed handoff workflow builder, live demo of Salesforce/Zendesk sync. Team engaged, asked good technical questions. Dylan focused heavily on pricing toward end of call.

questions_asked.question: Can we build custom automations or just use templates?
questions_asked.answer: Showed the workflow builder, confirmed they can customize. Dylan's team seemed pleased with flexibility.

questions_asked.question: How does this integrate with our existing Clari setup?
questions_asked.answer: Explained coexistence model, can pull data from Clari or run in parallel. Dylan noted they might just sunset Clari.

questions_asked.question: What's your actual pricing for 9 seats with the features we need?
questions_asked.answer: Quoted $10k for automations tier. Dylan went quiet, mentioned budget is tighter than expected.

objections: That's higher than I was hoping. I'm working with a budget closer to $6-7k.
next_steps: Send formal proposal with pricing options

touches.ai_summary: Sent proposal with three tiers: automations-only ($8k), automations + consulting ($10k), enterprise ($14k). Dylan responded asking if we can work with his budget constraints.

questions_asked.question: Is there flexibility on the automations + consulting tier? That's what we need but $10k is a stretch.
questions_asked.answer: Offered to schedule a call to discuss options and potential discount for faster decision.

touches.ai_summary: Negotiation call with Dylan. He confirmed budget ceiling of $6.5k, pushed hard on discount. Discussed value, ROI on time savings. Dylan emphasized he's new in role and needs a win but can't overspend. Agreed to bring revised pricing to leadership.

questions_asked.question: Is there anyone else who should be part of this conversation on budget?
questions_asked.answer: No, I own CS tooling budget. CRO trusts me on this but I need to stay within my allocated spend.

questions_asked.question: What's the best you can do on the automations + consulting package?
questions_asked.answer: Said I'd need to check with leadership, but hinted at potential to get closer to his range.

objections: I can't justify $10k. My budget is $6.5k max and I'm already stretching it.
next_steps: Rep to get approval for discount
Dylan to prepare internal business case

touches.ai_summary: Pricing call to present revised offer. Proposed $6.4k for automations + consulting (36% discount) as end-of-quarter special. Dylan receptive but wanted to confirm scope and contract terms.

questions_asked.question: Does this include the full implementation support we discussed?
questions_asked.answer: Confirmed yes, includes 20 hours of consulting for workflow setup and training.

questions_asked.question: Can you move forward this week to hit our quarter close?
questions_asked.answer: Dylan said he can make a decision but needs final contract to review terms.

next_steps: Dylan to review and return signed by Friday

touches.ai_summary: Final negotiation call to walk through contract. Dylan had questions on renewal terms, auto-renewal clause, and offboarding process. Adjusted contract language to address concerns. Dylan confirmed he'll sign by end of week.

questions_asked.question: What happens at renewal? Does price go back to $10k?
questions_asked.answer: Explained pricing holds for year one, renewal at standard rate unless we negotiate new terms. Dylan accepted.

questions_asked.question: Can we remove the auto-renewal and make it opt-in?
questions_asked.answer: Agreed to modify to 60-day notice required for non-renewal instead of auto-renewal.

questions_asked.question: Do you need anyone else to review the contract?
questions_asked.answer: No, I can sign. Just want to make sure I understand the terms clearly.

next_steps: Dylan to sign contract by Friday
Kickoff call scheduled for following Monday
